question.title: POI XSSF and SXSSF
question.body:
I am writing an application to use Apache POI to read over 65536 records from oracle table and write to Excel .xlsx via Apache POI library XSSF and SXSSF.

However, the error "out of memory" pops up. We have tried the following ways but the problem still occurs.

  • Load 66000 records into memory and write to .xlsx file.

    • Larger heap size java.exe –Xmx4096m –Xmx4096m –XX:MaxPermSize=256m is applied

    Result: It takes about an hour to run and the file cannot be created.

  • Process 66000 records by region

    • load each region records' of a season from an Oracle table into memory
    • append each region records from memory to a single .xlsx file
    • larger heap size java.exe –Xmx4096m –Xmx4096m –XX:MaxPermSize=256m is applied

    Result: When it processes about 30000 records, the Java memory error still pops up.

Except rewriting the program by using XML, is there any chance of fixing the memory error issue of Apache POI XSSF and SXSSF?

answers.body:
You need to do everything in chunk, so read in chunk and write in chunk. XSSF is not recommended here unless it is a small file (for template e.g.).

SXSSF allows you to set a fix rowAccessWindowSize so you can use that to write in chunk:

SXSSFWorkbook outputWorkbook = new SXSSFWorkbook(100);

For reading in chunk, there are a couple options but I recommend adding a new library if you want to keep it simple and straightforward. You can choose 1 of the below depended on your dependencies' version.
https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/com.monitorjbl/xlsx-streamer
https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/com.github.pjfanning/excel-streaming-reader

Workbook inputWorkbook = StreamingReader.builder()
        .rowCacheSize(100)      // Number of rows in memory
        .bufferSize(4096)       // Buffer size used when reading input
        .open(fileInputStream); // InputStream or File for XLSX file

If you prefer the harder way:
How to use POI SXSSF to read a large spreadsheet
